title: Venice Art Show Jury Won't Give Prizes to Russia or Israel
summary: Imagine you're at the biggest art show in the world, the Venice Biennale in Italy. It happens every two years, and countries from all over set up their own special rooms called pavilions. But this year, something huge happened. The jury that picks the winners said they won't give awards to any country whose leader is charged with crimes against humanity (really serious crimes like war crimes) by the International Criminal Court (ICC). That means Russia and Israel can't win any prizes. The jury is made up of five women. They said they don't want to connect artists with the bad actions of their governments. Many artists in the show support this. Russia is coming back to the Biennale after missing it twice since the war in Ukraine started. Israel is also there, but a lot of people are angry about it. Nearly 10,000 people signed a letter saying Russia shouldn't be allowed. The European Union even said it might stop giving the Biennale about $2.3 million. Some groups say the jury should have named Israel and Russia directly. But the jury says they're following their conscience.
